name: cosign
title: cosign
summary: Code signing/transparency for containers and binaries (program)
description:
Code signing/transparency for containers and binaries (program)
Signing OCI containers (and other artifacts) using Sigstore
.
Cosign supports:
.
* "Keyless signing" with the Sigstore public good Fulcio certificate
authority and Rekor transparency log (default)
* Hardware and KMS signing
* Signing with a cosign generated encrypted private/public keypair
* Container Signing, Verification and Storage in an OCI registry.
* Bring-your-own PKI
.
This package contains the command-line tool cosign.

arguments.description: Verify an attestation on the supplied container image
arguments.raw: -attestation  Verify an attestation on the supplied container image

arguments.description: Attest the supplied blob. bundle Interact with a Sigstore protobuf bundle
arguments.raw: -blob  Attest the supplied blob. bundle Interact with a Sigstore protobuf bundle

arguments.description: signing-config Interact with a Sigstore pro-
arguments.raw: -encoded  signing-config Interact with a Sigstore pro-

arguments.description: a Sigstore protobuf trusted root verify Verify a signature on the supplied
arguments.raw: -root  a Sigstore protobuf trusted root verify Verify a signature on the supplied

arguments.description: This cosign was not built with piv-tool
arguments.raw: -tool  This cosign was not built with piv-tool

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cosign because Code signing/transparency for containers and binaries (program).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cosign as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cosign with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cosign --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
